content: I increasingly prefer data-driven UI systems over deeply nested component architectures.
It reduces complexity when scaling applications and makes state flow more predictable.
In systems like Svelte or React, too many components often create abstraction noise instead of clarity.
I increasingly prefer data-driven UI systems over deeply nested component architectures.
It reduces complexity when scaling applications and makes state flow more predictable.
In systems like Svelte or React, too many components often create abstraction noise instead of clarity.
Systems are not just tools for execution, but also tools for thought.
The structure of your code influences how you think about problems.
